content.problemStatement:
Chef found a strange string yesterday - a string of signs **s**, where each sign is either a **'<'**, **'='** or a **'>'**. Let N be the length of this string. Chef wants to insert N + 1 positive integers into this sequence and make it valid. A valid sequence is a sequence where every sign is preceded and followed by an integer, and the signs are correct. That is, if a sign '<' is preceded by the integer a and followed by an integer b, then a should be less than b. Likewise for the other two signs as well. 

Chef can take some positive integers in the range **[1, P]** and use a number in the range as many times as he wants.

Help Chef find the minimum possible **P** with which he can create a valid sequence.
constraints: Sum of |s| over all test cases ≤ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string s consisting only of characters '<', '=', '>'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum possible integer P.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Here are some possible valid sequences which can be formed with the minimum P for each of the test cases: 1 < 2 < 3 < 4, 1 < 2 > 1 < 2, 1 < 2 = 2 > 1, 1 < 2 = 2 < 3

solution.approach:
We need to assign a positive integer a_i to each of the N+1 positions (0‑based) such that for every sign s[i] the relation between a_i and a_{i+1} holds. The smallest possible maximum value P can be obtained by a classic two‑pass DP:
1. Left to right pass computes L[i] – the smallest value that position i can take considering only '<' and '=' constraints to its left. Initialise L[0]=1. If s[i-1]=='<', then L[i]=L[i-1]+1; if s[i-1]=='=', L[i]=L[i-1]; otherwise (s[i-1]=='>') L[i]=1.
2. Right to left pass computes R[i] – the smallest value that position i can take considering only '>' and '=' constraints to its right. Initialise R[N]=1. If s[i]=='>', then R[i]=R[i+1]+1; if s[i]=='=', R[i]=R[i+1]; otherwise R[i]=1.
3. The value at position i must be at least max(L[i],R[i]). The answer P is the maximum of these minima over all positions.
The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and O(N) additional memory (which can be reduced to O(1) by keeping only the current values, but O(N) is well within limits).
solution.timeComplexity: O(|s|) per test case, overall O(total length of all strings).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|s|) for the two auxiliary arrays (can be reduced to O(1)).
